tagline: Work with Freshdesk support records from the command line.

global_flags.name: connection
global_flags.summary: Use a saved Freshdesk connector credential.

global_flags.name: max-bytes
global_flags.summary: Maximum direct-read response bytes; capped at 1048576.

global_flags.name: plan
global_flags.summary: Preview or approve an existing reverse-ETL command plan.

global_flags.name: preview
global_flags.summary: Preview a reverse-ETL command plan before approval.

global_flags.name: approve
global_flags.summary: Approval token for executing an existing reverse-ETL command plan.

global_flags.name: confirm
global_flags.summary: Typed confirmation for destructive Freshdesk write plans.

flags.name: updated-since
flags.summary: Use the configured start_date/incremental lower bound for ticket updates.

commands.risk: Freshdesk Delete a Group (DELETE /admin/groups/{id}); requires reverse ETL plan, preview, approval, and execute.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, and execute; destructive/high-risk Freshdesk writes also require typed confirmation.

help_topics.name: freshdesk
help_topics.summary: Freshdesk streams, bounded direct reads, and gated reverse-ETL commands.

help_topics.name: freshdesk-writes
help_topics.summary: Freshdesk write commands use named reverse-ETL actions with plan, preview, approval, execute, and destructive confirmation where required.

help_topics.name: freshdesk-direct-reads
help_topics.summary: Freshdesk direct reads are fixed connector-relative GET operations with JSON output capped at 1 MiB.
